prestazioni: Engine swap to M127.982 2.5L inline-6 with forged Mahle pistons (87.5mm bore, 78.8mm stroke), Carrillo H-beam connecting rods, and a custom Schrick 272° camshaft for high-lift, long-duration performance (264° intake, 272° exhaust, 11.5mm lift). Paired with a Kugelfischer mechanical fuel injection system recalibrated by Kienzle for 12.5:1 compression ratio, producing ~220 HP at 6,800 RPM with a redline of 7,200 RPM.

Bilstein B16 PSS10 coilovers with custom valving for the W113 chassis, featuring 46mm monotube dampers, adjustable compression/rebound (10-way), and progressive-rate Eibach ERS springs (front: 220 lb/in, rear: 180 lb/in) to lower ride height by 35mm while retaining classic Pagoda geometry. Front sway bar upgraded to a 22mm Addco unit with polyurethane bushings for reduced body roll.

Custom 3.92:1 limited-slip differential (ZF Type 220) with cryo-treated gears and a Quaife ATB helical differential, paired with a Getrag 265 5-speed manual transmission (from the W114 250CE) with a short-throw shifter (Hurst Competition/Plus) and a Sachs Performance twin-disc clutch (240mm, 750 Nm torque capacity) for aggressive launches.
estetica: Full carbon fiber body kit by RWB x Pagoda (Rauh-Welt Begriff), including widened fenders (30mm front, 40mm rear), a custom front splitter with integrated tow hooks, and a ducktail spoiler (120mm height) for aerodynamic balance. Painted in Mercedes-Benz Classic Signal Red (DB 568) with a matte clear coat by Spies Hecker.

BBS RS-GT 15x7 (front) and 15x8 (rear) forged wheels with a 4x112 bolt pattern, ET25 offset, and polished lips, wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires (195/50/15 front, 225/50/15 rear). Wheel arches rolled and flared by a specialist using a custom hydraulic press to accommodate the wider track.

Interior retrimmed in black Nappa leather by Recaro with red contrast stitching, featuring custom bucket seats (Recaro SRD-E) with integrated 4-point Schroth harnesses, a MOMO Prototipo steering wheel (380mm, suede), and a custom aluminum gauge pod (VDO) housing a 10,000 RPM tachometer, oil temperature/pressure gauges, and a boost gauge (for potential future forced induction).
